summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js exfiltrates host info via postinstall
details: This package impersonates the legitimate @solana/web3.js library under the @solana-labs scope. Its package.json declares a postinstall hook (`"postinstall": "node install.js"`) that automatically runs install.js on `npm install`. install.js collects host identifiers (os.hostname(), process.platform), invokes execSync and curl to gather additional environment data, and POSTs the result over HTTPS to a hardcoded endpoint. The legitimate Solana web3 SDK (`@solana/web3.js`) does not ship a postinstall script or any install-time network beacon — this package's install.js exists solely to fingerprint and report installer machines. Installing this package causes immediate, non-consensual disclosure of host metadata to an attacker-controlled destination on every developer or CI machine that runs `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
